body_txt: Some characters are defined by liking a certain type of food or drink, such as candy , coffee , or booze . In this case, it's spicy food. These characters have a high tolerance for the heat and are well-known for eating dishes that would turn most people into Fire Breathing Diners , usually with little to no reaction. At the extreme, these characters chow down on dangerously hot foods and dishes whenever possible and relish the burn, to the point that they may even ask for extra spice. For them, Blazing Inferno Hellfire Sauce is the elixir of the gods and nothing else will suffice. They might even drink the stuff . Bystanders may watch them eat with mingled amazement and horror, provided the fumes coming from the food don't send them fleeing. These characters' spice tolerance is often measured against other characters, such as shrugging off foods that leave others in agony from the heat or foiling a prankster who has liberally doused their food with hot sauce by eating it and not batting an eye. This can also make them dangerous in the kitchen, as they have a tendency to not take other people's lower spice tolerances into account when cooking. Justified for some characters due to being constantly exposed to such food as part of their cultural cuisine, natural resistance to heat, a reduced sense of taste , or not having the necessary taste receptors. Opposite Trope to Plain Palate and White-Bread Palate . Compare Masochist's Meal . Not related to Spice of Life .
